situation: International student at TU Munich applied for DAK student health insurance. Submitted an enrollment letter from the university's international office, but DAK required the official Immatrikulationsbescheinigung with a current semester stamp.
rejection_reason: Enrollment confirmation from the International Office was not accepted — it lacked the official university seal and current semester enrollment stamp required by §5 SGB V for student membership status.
what_worked: Obtained the official Immatrikulationsbescheinigung directly from TU Munich's Studierendenkanzlei via the online student portal. Document included all required stamps. Resubmitted and DAK confirmed membership the same day.
what_failed: Submitting an unofficial enrollment confirmation email. Using a screenshot from the university's online portal. Asking the International Office to add a stamp to their letter.
